text:
Market today: Mixed but tech has a pulse. Micron's blowout earnings are lifting the chip space in premarket — that's a direct tailwind for your AVGO and SMH today. But JPMorgan is flagging that retail is pulling back from margin and options, which is exactly your setup. High-vol, margin-amplified tech book in a cautious tape is a dangerous combo.

Your holdings: Three need eyes. AVGO (-12.45%, your largest) is bleeding on margin — every down day costs you more than it looks. PLTR (-12.77%) is close enough to feeling wrong; no fundamental catalyst in today's data to bail you out. SPAL (-14.48%) is a 2x leveraged decay product — it's already near your $19 stop and has no recovery mechanism built in; treat it as burning rope. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — tiny sizing only.

Watch: (1) How chip names respond to Micron's pop — does AVGO catch a bid? (2) Friday's massive volume event could whipsaw everything fast.

Bottom line: Your margin exposure on two double-digit losers (AVGO, PLTR) is the real fire — Micron's pop is your window to reassess, not relax.
